comments.text: A partial result survives for asymptotically maximum Sidon sets (those with $\operatorname{diam}(A) = (1+o(1))|A|^2$). Corrected writeup at https://leon2k2k2k.github.io/erdos153_corrected.pdf. The idea: by Pikhurko's uniformity lemma, an asymptotically maximum Sidon set is equidistributed in its ambient interval, which forces $A+A$ to have triangular density $\varphi(u) = \min(u, 1-u)$ in $[0, 2D]$. Near the boundary this density vanishes linearly, so $A+A$ is sparse and the gaps grow. A localization inequality (CauchyвАУSchwarz on disjoint windows) turns this sparsity into a lower bound on $\sum d_i^2$: the contribution from the edge is a Riemann sum for $\int_\varepsilon^{1/2} du/u = \log(1/(2\varepsilon)) \to \infty$. The case $\liminf \operatorname{diam}(A_n)/n^2 > 1$ remains open вАФ Pikhurko uniformity requires asymptotic maximality. Found with the help of GPT-5.5 + Rethlas; verified by hand.
comments.meta: leon2k2k2k вАФ 00:13 on 20 May 2026 рЯСН 1 рЯУЭ 0 рЯ§Ц 0

comments.text: Edit: The proof below is incorrect. Here is a short proof of the statement using the shifted-intersection bound $|(A+A) \cap (A+A+d)| \leq 2|A|^{3/2}$ that Bloom extracted from the proof of Theorem 2 of [ESS94] on the #152 forum thread. Write $A+A = {s_1 < \cdots < s_t}$, $d_i = s_{i+1}-s_i$, $n = |A|$, so $t = n(n+1)/2$. Fix $R \geq 1$. Each index $i$ with $d_i = d$ gives a distinct element $s_{i+1} \in (A+A) \cap (A+A+d)$, so summing the BloomвАУESS94 bound over $d \in \{1, \ldots, R\}$ yields $\#\{i : 1 \leq d_i \leq R\} \leq 2R\,n^{3/2} = o(t)$ as $n \to \infty$. Hence at least $(1-o(1))\,t$ gaps satisfy $d_i > R$, so $Q(A) \geq R^2(1-o(1))$; taking $R \to \infty$ gives $Q(A_n) \to \infty$. Writeup at https://leon2k2k2k.github.io/erdos153.pdf. Found with the help of GPT-5.5 + Rethlas; verified by hand. ---

comments.text: Not sure if this is interesting enough to comment, but this conjecture is immediate in the вАЬvery sparse relative to its spanвАЭ regime, and does not use the Sidon property at all. Let $A$ be any finite set of integers, $|A|=k$, and write $A+A=\{s_1<\dots<s_t\}$ with gaps $d_i=s_{i+1}-s_i$. Let $D:=\operatorname{Diam}(A)=\max(A)-\min(A)$. Then $s_t-s_1=2D$. We have $$\frac1t\sum_{i=1}^{t-1} d_i^2 \ge \frac{16D^2}{k^2(k+1)^2}$$ by CauchyвАУSchwarz. In particular, if $D\gg k^2$ (e.g. for $A\subset[1,n]$ with $\operatorname{Diam}(A)\asymp n$ and $k=o(\sqrt n)$), then the mean square consecutive gap $\to\infty$.
comments.meta: Sayan Dutta вАФ 16:13 on 15 Feb 2026 рЯСН 0 рЯУЭ 0 рЯ§Ц 0

comments.text: Interesting. I guess when you wrote $D\gg k^2$, you meant $D/k^2\to\infty$ as $k\to\infty$. Since dense Sidon sets with $k$ elements have diameter about $k^2$, one needs to exploit more about the structure of $A+A$.
